methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ in total funding, including a C$23M tranche from the Quantum Champions fund in December 2025. The broader quantum computing sector comprises 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms, anchored by peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. A review of same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within quantum computing yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median analysis. The sector's nascent stage and heterogeneous business models—spanning superconducting, trapped-ion, and photonic architectures—limit meaningful peer clustering by development or commercial maturity. All-sector median approaches would obscure material differences in technical approach and revenue trajectory.

Without sufficient narrow-set peer data, traditional triangulation cannot be responsibly applied. A credible valuation would require either (1) expanded dataset including earlier funding rounds of marquee peers (IonQ, Quantinuum series history); (2) revenue or customer-concentration metrics for Nord Quantique relative to private peers; or (3) explicit use of venture capital method benchmarking to 10-year exit scenarios. The C$40M+ raised to date provides a hard floor, but further valuation support hinges on access to additional comparable transaction or performance data.
